name: The Champion Plan for 100K to 100 Miles
author: David Roche & Megan Roche, MD PhD
description: A 16-week plan for 100K to 100 mile races. Low end of distance ranges targets intermediate athletes; high end targets very advanced athletes with a big base. Midpoint values are used as defaults.

sessions.notes: Rest. Heat ideal, like hot tub or sauna 20-30 min. Light x-train or a hike is good on any rest day. I like full rest though, with some mobility work and core.

sessions.pace_description: Easy effort, Z1/Z2. Smooth, relaxed, light on feet.
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second hills fast. On easy running, think smooth, relaxed, and light on feet. Ideally Z1/Z2, but it’s ok for HR to get higher on ups. Do easy runs by effort, and it’s ok to flow when it flows! On the hill strides, do them anytime after halfway, easing into them to avoid a pulled muscle, but you can push as fast as you can go without using sprinting form. Jog down the hill for recovery. If you have a treadmill, you can add 10-15 minutes on the uphill treadmill after any run at 10-15% grade for fatigue resistance! That can be weight vest hiking as well. Do the Core Snack routine 1-2 times every day!

sessions.pace_description: 5K effort on 90s hills, 3K effort on 45s hills, marathon effort on moderate section
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 6 x 90 second hills mod/hard (think 5k effort) with run down recovery after each, 6 x 45 second hills faster (think 3k effort), 1-2 miles moderate (think marathon effort), 1-2 miles easy The hills will engage top-end power, the moderate running after will help the body shuttle lactate at higher speeds Optional easy double or x-train. Any double can be the uphill treadmill, or a hike with a weight vest. Doubles are 25-45 min, with 30 min being a sweet spot, and they can be VERY easy. If your race involves lots of hiking, weight vest hiking at 15-20% grade would be ideal to work into the plan as you get closer to race day, working on muscular endurance.

Light Strength (like Mountain Legs). Do the Mountain Legs right after you finish your run on strength days! You can do a different routine that works for you as well on days with Mountain legs (like Ultra Legs), or add squats.

sessions.pace_description: Easy effort, Z2 on easy sections. Push on fast intervals.
sessions.notes: 1-2 hours easy x-train with 8 x 30 seconds fast/2 min easy. The x-train days are ideally bike, elliptical, uphill treadmill running or weight vest hiking, stairmill, or some combination of similar activities. Aim to keep easy in Z2, rather than extremely low effort. On fast pushes, do a bigger bike gear or higher elliptical resistance and push! You can sub in a very easy run for any x-train day if you are 100% healthy, or split the day into a double. The usual Tuesday mileage ranges are ideal if running.

4-10 miles very easy. Aerobic recovery day! Only advanced athletes should go farther than 6 miles on Friday, since you want to be fully recovered for weekend Heat ideal. Heat can be hot tub, sauna, or similar!

10-16 miles easy/mod, progressing to steady effort. On these easy/mod runs, it’s ok to let effort wander up. Practice fueling with plenty of carbs and hydration! Some vert is good, but choose whatever route seems most fun to you Ideally do your long runs on terrain with some climbing and descending! Vert is your friend, and vert is more important than mileage in most cases (even high volume athletes can prioritize shorter runs with vert). Think quick feet on the downhills, using every downhill as an opportunity to work on that skill
Mountain Legs

sessions.pace_description: Easy effort with fast hill strides
sessions.notes: 6-14 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second hills fast or easy/mod x-train. Sunday is a flex day--you could even go for a long bike or ski or hike! Or mix up a run and uphill treadmill!
Mountain legs + squats (on Sundays, add 2 x 10 reps of squats) + back extensions (2 x 20), engaging glutes. You can also continue any strength program that works for you!
Rest. Heat ideal. Very advanced athletes could jog, but only if super high mileage 80+ mpw!

6-14 miles easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy. On these flatter strides, ease into them even more since injury risk is higher. The top end of the range is only for high mileage athletes. Most athletes will thrive in the middle of the range as a max, particularly the day before workouts when you want to feel fresh.

sessions.pace_description: 5K effort on fast intervals, 50K effort on easy/mod section
sessions.notes: 2-4 miles easy warm-up, 16 x 1 min fast/1 min easy (think 5k effort on fasts), 2-3 miles easy/mod to mod (think 50k), 1-2 miles easy. After workouts is the most ideal time for 10-15 min post-run uphill treadmill. Speed workout classic! Just building up speed stimulus Optional easy double or x-train
Mountain Legs

sessions.pace_description: Easy effort with structured fast intervals
sessions.notes: 1-2 hours easy x-train with 5 x (4 x 1 min fast/1 min easy) with 2 min easy between sets. We want to build some x-train power before it becomes mostly aerobic later. If you’re doing uphill treadmill or jogging on this day, do not do any of the structured workouts--just keep it unstructured.
Mountain Legs after your run (per the plan).
4-12 miles very easy Heat ideal. You can add passive heat on other days as well. Passive heat is low stress, so add it freely, but not on double days

sessions.pace_description: Easy/mod with 20 min mod/hard tempo near the start
sessions.notes: 13-18 miles easy/mod (20 min mod/hard around 1-hour effort near the start of the run). On the tempo, think relaxed, with permission to push a bit more near the end! The tempo would be best on a net uphill to reduce impact forces
Mountain legs + squats + back extensions. Strength a day earlier when Tuesday workouts the following week! But you can be flexible with timing

sessions.pace_description: Very easy with fast hill strides
sessions.notes: 6-14 miles very easy plus 6 x 30 second hills fast or easy/mod x-train. The stress of the first 2 weeks likely adding up a bit now!
Mountain Legs
Rest. Heat ideal

sessions.pace_description: Easy effort with fast hill strides
sessions.notes: 2-4 miles easy warm-up, 2-3 x (4 x 2 min fast/1 min easy) with 2 min easy between sets (think 10k to 5k effort), 2 miles moderate (think marathon), 1-2 miles easy. Really big and hard session! For the big workouts, there is a range of sets so that lower volume athletes don’t overdo it. Remember, the goal is not to go as hard or fast as you can, but to run smoothly and finish feeling strong! Always err on the side of less intensity, since health and the daily aerobic work is most important for long-term growth. Optional easy double or x-train Mountain Legs

sessions.pace_description: 10K to 5K effort on intervals, marathon effort on moderate section
sessions.notes: 6-14 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second hills fast. Keep these miles very easy post-workout
Mountain Legs after your run (per the plan).
1-2 hours easy x-train with 3-4 x 3/2/1 min fast with 1 min easy between intervals and 3 min easy between sets The final tougher x-train workout for a bit as the body adapts!
Mountain Legs
4-12 miles very easy Heat ideal

sessions.pace_description: Easy with 1 mile harder near the end
sessions.notes: 16-20 miles easy with 1 mile harder near the end. Pure aerobic day, with the faster mile being a fatigue resistance stimulus. Fuel these long runs like the race to work on fatigue resistance. Generally, aim for 75+ grams of carbs per hour on long runs
Mountain Legs
8-14 miles easy or easy/mod x-train. Sunday x-train days could always be long adventures or hikes!
Mountain legs + squats + back extensions
Rest. Heat ideal

sessions.pace_description: Easy with strides. Smooth and strong.
sessions.notes: 8-14 miles easy with 5 x 30 seconds fast/2 min easy. Ease into the strides, thinking smooth and strong!

Uphill treadmill at 8-10% ideal, though this workout can also be done outside, just cut the total intervals in half given downhill running! 15-20 min easy warm-up, 12-15 x 3 min around 1-hour effort with 90 seconds easy recovery after each, 15-20 min easy cooldown. Uphill treadmill threshold workouts allow for very big sessions with little impact or risk, and will be key to this plan. If doing these hill workouts outside, err on the side of more relaxed! Optional easy double or x-train
Mountain Legs

1-2 hours easy x-train. Make sure this is quality Z2 if you’re doing a non-impact activity like cycling. Now would be a great time to work on more weight vest uphill hiking if you’re doing races with lots of hiking!
Mountain Legs

sessions.pace_description: Very easy with short hill strides
sessions.notes: 6-14 miles very easy plus 5 x 20 second hills Heat ideal

sessions.pace_description: Easy/mod with 20 min mod/hard tempo, finishing harder
sessions.notes: 12-18 miles easy/mod with 20 min mod/hard around 1-hour effort, finishing harder (do the tempo near the start of the run after the warm-up), plus 5 min harder near the end. This run will tax glycogen reserves, so fuel with major focus! The tempo after the warm-up makes the distance of the run play longer
Mountain Legs
10-16 miles easy, progressing effort to steady as you go, plus 6 x 30 second hills at the end or easy/mod x-train
Mountain Legs + squats + back extensions
Rest. Heat ideal

sessions.pace_description: Easy with strides. Smooth and quick.
sessions.notes: 8-14 miles easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy. Think smooth and quick on strides, with each one being an opportunity to improve speed

2-3 miles easy warm-up, 3-5 x 800/400/200 fast on the track with 400 easy after 800s, 200 after 400s, 400 after 200s (think 10k/5k/3k effort on the intervals), 4 x 200 fast/200 slow (push), 2-3 miles easy cool-down. First track workout! If you don’t want to go to the track, do 3-4 x 3/2/1 min fast with 1 min easy between intervals and 3 min easy between sets (think 10k/5k/3k effort), plus 4 x 30 seconds fast/90 seconds easy (push). Track workout helps solidify speed gains! Optional easy double or x-train
Mountain Legs
1.5-2.5 hours easy x-train
Mountain Legs
6-14 miles very easy Heat ideal

sessions.pace_description: Easy/mod with 1-hour mod to mod/hard progression
sessions.notes: 16-20 miles easy/mod (1 hour mod to mod/hard, starting at marathon effort and progressing as you go). The power hour will be a bit tough, embrace that space since it will have the best translation to race readiness! You can also do training races any week! If you have any trail half marathons or similar events near you, they can replace long runs, just keep the Friday shorter
Mountain Legs

sessions.pace_description: Easy, pure recovery style. Light on feet.
sessions.notes: 10-16 miles easy, pure recovery style. Think light on feet and relaxed!
Mountain Legs + squats + back extensions
Rest. Heat ideal
6-12 miles easy plus 5 x 20 second hills. Aerobic down week ahead, dialing back stress a bit!
10-13 miles easy/mod to mod, progressing effort as you go, 2 miles easy cool-down. Just settling in with steady running, which will be key in longer races! Optional easy double or x-train
Mountain Legs
1.5-2.5 hours easy x-train
Mountain Legs
4-12 miles very easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy Heat ideal
10-16 miles easy over hills. Pure aerobic day! Let’s have this weekend be nice and relaxed! If your race has a ton of vert, now is a great time to start focusing on vert profiles like the race
Mountain Legs
8-14 miles easy over hills or easy/mod x-train. Pure aerobic day!
Mountain Legs + squats + back extensions
Rest. Heat ideal
8-14 miles easy with 4 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy

2-3 miles easy warm-up, 2-3 x (4 x 600 fast with 200 easy between each) and 600 easy between sets. Think 10k effort to start on the intervals, progressing the effort as you go! Finish with 4 x 200 fast/200 slow, pushing a bit, and 2-3 mile easy cooldown. If you aren’t on the track, do 2-4 miles easy warm-up, 8-10 x 2 min fast (think 10k) with 90 seconds float recovery (just above normal easy), 2 miles steady (think 50k), 1-2 miles easy Optional easy double or x-train
Mountain Legs
1.5-2.5 hours easy/mod x-train. These Thursdays are all about solid aerobic stimuli!
Mountain Legs
6-14 miles very easy Heat ideal

sessions.pace_description: Easy/mod with 4-7 x 6 min threshold intervals
sessions.notes: 16-20 miles easy/mod with 4-7 x 6 min around 1-hour effort with 2 min easy recovery. Big threshold session, ideally on rolling terrain. Err on the side of easier effort on the intervals for true threshold!
Mountain Legs + squats + back extensions

sessions.pace_description: Easy with steep hill strides at the end
sessions.notes: 10-16 miles easy plus 4 x 30 second steep hills at the end or easy/mod x-train
Rest. Heat ideal

Uphill treadmill at 8-10% ideal, though this workout can also be done outside! 15-20 min easy warm-up, 8-12 x 4 min around 1-hour effort with 90 seconds easy recovery after each, 15-20 min easy cooldown. If you do this hill workout outside, cut the intervals in half, something like 4-6 x 4 min hills Optional easy double or x-train
Mountain Legs
8-16 miles easy plus 6 x 30 second hills

sessions.pace_description: Easy effort. Recovery before bigger weekend run.
sessions.notes: 1-2 hours easy x-train. Nice recovery before bigger weekend run. Weight vest hiking would be a great option if your race involves lots of hiking!
6-14 miles very easy plus 5 x 20 second hills Heat ideal

sessions.pace_description: Easy/mod with fast downhills, harder finish on uphill
sessions.notes: 20-25 miles easy/mod with some very fast downhills on steep terrain, plus 5 min harder near the end on an uphill. Aim to get sore from this one, dialing in eccentric muscle contractions that will be good for durability. If you’re doing major vert, even advanced athletes can be at the low end of the range
Mountain Legs
10-16 miles easy/mod flow on flatter/rolling terrain, practicing settling into a Z2/Z3 rhythm or easy/mod x-train
Mountain Legs + squats + back extensions
Rest. Heat ideal

8-14 miles easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy. Dial back volume this week if you’re feeling beat up at all--we’re trying to have a slight overreach here for adaptation on the down week next week!

sessions.pace_description: 10K effort progressing to harder on 800m repeats
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 8-10 x 800 fast with 400 easy recovery after each (think 10k effort to start, pushing a bit as you go and ending harder), 1-2 miles steady, 1-2 miles easy. Quality track session before down week next week! If not on the track, do 2-3 mi easy warm-up, 5-8 x 3 min fast (think 10k effort) with 2 min easy recovery, 1-2 mi steady, 1-2 mi easy. Lactate shuttling and speed tuning day 10 days out from a solid effort!
Mountain Legs + squats + back extensions
1.5-2.5 hours easy/mod x-train
Mountain Legs
6-14 miles very easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy Heat ideal

sessions.pace_description: Easy. Very easy and relaxed, pure aerobic.
sessions.notes: 12-20 miles easy. Very easy and relaxed, pure aerobic activation!
Mountain Legs

sessions.pace_description: Easy with steep hill strides at the end
sessions.notes: 6-14 miles easy plus 6 x 30 second steep hills at the end or easy/mod x-train
Mountain Legs
Rest or easy jog. An easy jog would be good if you want to keep volume higher! Heat ideal

sessions.pace_description: 1-hour effort on threshold, 3K effort on hills
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 3 x 5 min around 1-hour effort with 1 min easy recovery, 5 min easy, 4 x 45 second slight hills fast (think 3k effort) with 75 seconds easy recovery, 2-3 miles easy cool-down. A light tune-up pre weekend effort, practicing the taper and making sure it works for you!
Mountain Legs
5-10 miles easy No strength the rest of the week!
Rest and recovery. Want to go into this big weekend effort feeling recovered and ready! Heat ideal

sessions.pace_description: Easy to steady with light hill strides
sessions.notes: 3-8 miles easy to steady plus 4 x 30 sec light hills around 3k/5k effort.

sessions.pace_description: Moderate race effort with full fueling approach
sessions.notes: BIG DAY!!!! This can be a marathon, 50k, or even 50 mile race. If no racing, aim for a moderate 50k on terrain like the race, pushing moderately hard and with full fueling approach. You can move this backward to 8-10 weeks pre-race if there’s a race or 100k, just move around weeks accordingly. Goal: get very sore! Weeks 10 and 11 can be moved to accommodate a training race
CELEBRATION! You should be quite sore from this one in a good way. Make sure you eat a lot the next few days
Rest. Heat ideal
Rest. This is a mid-cycle rest block, with a down week ahead to solidify gains Heat ideal

4-10 miles easy. Really ease back in! Now is when to start focused heat suit training if your race might be very hot and you aren’t running in that type of heat consistently. In that case, aim for 2 x 30-60 minutes with a heat suit each week within the context of training, keeping heart rate in Z2 or easier. Don’t fry yourself! Heat suit could be on doubles or replacing some easy running.
1.5-2.5 hours easy/mod x-train with 10 x 30 seconds fast/2 min easy
Mountain Legs
4-10 miles easy Heat ideal
10-16 mi easy. Pure aerobic day!
Mountain Legs

sessions.pace_description: Easy with fast hills at the end
sessions.notes: 8-14 mi easy plus 4 x 30 sec hills fast near the end or easy/mod x-train. You want to get to the end of this week feeling fresh!
Mountain Legs + squats + back extensions
Rest. Heat ideal
8-14 miles easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy

sessions.pace_description: 5K effort on track intervals, marathon effort on moderate section
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 3-4 x (4 x 400 fast) with 1 min between intervals and 2 min between sets (think 5k effort on the intervals), 1-2 miles moderate (think marathon effort), 1-3 miles easy. A track workout to re-ignite speed mid-block! If off the track, do 2-4 miles easy, 16 x 1 min fast/1 min easy (think 5k effort on fasts), 2 miles moderate (think marathon effort), 1-3 miles easy. You might feel a bit tired here! Optional easy double or x-train
Mountain Legs
1.5-2.5 hours easy x-train
Mountain Legs
6-14 mi easy plus 4 x 30 second hills
16-20 mi easy/mod with 20 min mod/hard, pushing a bit as you go. Fuel this just like race day or even more! Let’s start really gut training, particularly with large amounts of fluid!
Mountain Legs + squats + back extensions
10-18 mi easy plus 8 x 30 sec steep hills at the end or easy/mod x-train. Fatigue resistance hills!
Mountain Legs
Rest. Heat ideal

Uphill treadmill at 8-10% ideal, though this workout can also be done outside! 15-20 min easy warm-up, 2-4 x 10 min around 1-hour effort with 2 min easy recovery after each, 2-4 x 5 min around 1-hour effort with 2 min easy recovery, 15-20 min easy cooldown. If you do a hill workout outside, the ideal session would be 4-6 x 5 min hills with run down recovery between each Optional easy double or x-train
10-18 miles easy plus 6 x 20 second slight hills No strength mid-week now as running stress gets higher!
1.5-2.5 hours easy/mod x-train
4-12 miles easy Heat ideal

sessions.pace_description: Easy/mod to mod on steep terrain with fast downhills
sessions.notes: 20-25 mi easy/mod to mod on steeper terrain with some fast downhills and 5 min hard near the end on tired legs. These harder downs will help prevent excess muscle damage from derailing race. The steeper the better (within reason), even for flat races
Mountain Legs + squats + back extensions

sessions.pace_description: Easy, progressing to steady over rolling terrain
sessions.notes: 10-20 mi easy, progressing to steady over rolling terrain
Rest. Heat ideal

Uphill treadmill at 8-10% ideal, though this workout can also be done outside! 15-20 min easy warm-up, 8-12 x 5 min around 1-hour effort with 1 min easy recovery after each, 15-20 min easy cooldown. This is a big one, embrace the space! If you’re outside, something like 5-8 x 5 min hills would be ideal, stepping up from last week. Optional easy double or x-train
Mountain Legs
10-20 miles easy. Aerobic endurance day! Ideally Z2 rather than pure Z1. Optional easy double or x-train for high volume athletes
1-2 hours easy x-train
Mountain Legs
6-14 miles easy plus 5 x 20 second hills Heat ideal

14-18 mi easy/mod to moderate (60-90 min mod/hard, just a nice effort to really open up the nervous system!) Only go beyond 1 hour if you’re high volume, feeling great, and recovering well. However, if you are feeling too tired, now is the time to dial back more and keep this run to 12 miles easy, starting the taper earlier
Mountain Legs after your run (per the plan).

sessions.pace_description: Easy, pure recovery. Light on feet.
sessions.notes: 10-16 mi easy or easy/mod x-train. Pure recovery mode, thinking light on feet!
Mountain Legs + squats + back extensions. Final squats day!
Rest. Heat ideal
6-10 mi easy plus 5 x 20 sec hills fast. Starting the full taper, keep it nice and relaxed going into Wed workout!

sessions.pace_description: Easy/mod over hills. Nice flow run.
sessions.notes: 8-12 mi easy/mod over hills. Just a nice flow run, with some serious vert if your race is steep! Optional easy double or x-train
Mountain Legs
46-60 min uphill treadmill easy Z2 or easy x-train 45-60 min. Pure aerobic day!
3-6 mi easy Heat ideal

sessions.pace_description: Easy/mod over hills. Final verty run.
sessions.notes: 10-14 mi easy/mod over hills. If your race is steep, this is the final verty run! Really practice GI training here, fueling like the race or more
Mountain Legs
4-8 mi easy. Pure chill day!
4-10 mi easy Z2

sessions.pace_description: 1-hour effort on threshold, fast hill strides
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 3 x 5 min around 1-hour effort with 1 min easy recovery, 5 min easy, 3 x 30 second slight hills fast, 2-3 miles easy cool-down. Repeating the tune-up workout that you used earlier in the cycle!
Mountain Legs to stay tuned up
Uphill treadmill Z2 40-60 min or 4-8 mi easy. Pure aerobic day! You can add an extra rest day here if you feel a lot better off of more rest!
Rest and recovery. Some light heat like sauna or hot tub would be good for blood volume!

sessions.pace_description: Easy to steady with light hill strides. Z2 ideal.
sessions.notes: 3-6 miles easy to steady plus 4 x 30 sec light hills around 3k/5k effort. Z2 running ideal for tune-up. HR will be jumpy and that’s ok!

sessions.pace_description: Z2 effort. Fuel and hydrate plenty. BELIEVE!
sessions.notes: 100 Mile or 100k Race! The big principles: think Z2 effort, fuel and hydrate plenty, and most of all: BELIEVE! You got this!
100 Mile or 100k Race! Celebration time!

strength_exercises.notes: Use bench at knee height. Drive through heel. Alternate legs each rep.
Keep torso upright, knee tracking over toes. Count each leg as one rep.
Hold wall for balance. Full range of motion — drop heel below step.
Hold for 45 seconds per set. Squeeze glutes, brace core, neutral spine.
Hold dumbbell or kettlebell at chest. Sit hips back, full depth.
Hinge at hips, soft knee bend. Feel stretch in hamstrings. Dumbbells or barbell.
Squeeze glutes at top, 2-second hold. Progress to single-leg when easy.
Hold for 30 seconds each side per set. Stack feet or stagger for stability.

heat_sessions.notes: 20-30 min sauna or hot tub. Ideal on rest days for passive heat acclimation.
20-30 min passive heat. Can do sauna, hot tub, or hot bath.
